div1.entryFree: Or, before, sooner than: would I had met my dearest foe in heaven or ever I had seen that day, Hml. I, 2, 183 (Ff ere I had ever seen). or I could make a prologue to my brains, they had begun the play, V, 2, 30 (Ff ere). I'll think he'll grant the tribute or look upon our Romans, Cymb. II, 4, 14. Particularly or ere (or e'er?) used in this sense: Tp. I, 2, 11. V, 103. Shr. IV, 5, 8. John IV, 3, 20. V, 6, 44. Mcb. IV, 3, 173. Hml. I, 2, 147. Lr. II, 4, 289. Cymb. III, 2, 67. V, 3, 50 (cf. Ere).
